name: 2.Jira IT Support RAG Agent - Dynamic Issue Type Demo

parameters.initialMessages: Hello, what can I help you with today?

nodes.name: When chat message received
nodes.notes: Main entry point for user chat interaction with the IT support agent.

nodes.name: Set Common Fields2
nodes.notes: Azure AI Search service/index names used by the vector-store tool.

nodes.name: Simple Memory
nodes.notes: Tracks previous messages for multi-turn context.

options.systemMessage:
You are an IT support agent responsible for helping users solve IT-related issues clearly and accurately for Acme Corporation.

Your behavior:
	• Always be helpful, specific, and concise.
	• Do not make assumptions. If you're not sure, say so.
	• Avoid vague answers. Give direct steps or clarifying questions.
	• Do not invent information.

How to respond:
 - Always assume the user is asking about specific internal company information.
	• If the vector store returns nothing useful, say: "I'm not sure. I couldn't find any internal documentation about that."

Tools:
	• Use the vector store to search for answers to company-specific questions.
	• Use memory to maintain conversation context across turns.
	• If a request needs escalation, create a Jira ticket. When you do, you MUST decide the Issue Type yourself based on the nature of the question:
		- Story: a feature request or improvement idea
		- Task: everything else (a routine how-to request, administrative ask, or a broken/error report)
	Do not ask the user which type to use - infer it from their message.

Example:
User: How do I connect to the VPN?
	• If vector search returns relevant info: "According to internal docs: [quote or summary]."
	• If unsure: "I don't have an answer for that and couldn't find anything in the docs. Please reach out to IT directly."

parameters.content: ## 1. Create the Azure AI Search Index (run once, manual trigger)

parameters.toolDescription: Create a Jira ticket to escalate an IT issue. Automatically picks the Jira Issue Type (Task / Story) based on what kind of question this is - do not ask the user which type, decide it yourself from context.

nodes.name: Create Jira Ticket (Dynamic Type)
nodes.notes: Dynamic version: calls Jira REST API directly (not the native Jira node) so the issue type can be chosen by name (Bug/Task/Story) via $fromAI instead of a fixed numeric ID.

parameters.content:
## Use Case: Jira IT Support RAG Agent in Practice

Upload internal documents → generate vectors with Azure OpenAI Embeddings → store them in Azure AI Search → AI Agent answers questions through semantic retrieval → automatically creates and escalates Jira tickets when needed.

Difference from the base template: the Jira escalation tool now selects a **different Issue Type (Task or Story)** based on the nature of the question, instead of always creating one fixed type. (This project’s available work types are Epic, Story, Subtask, and Task—there is no Bug type.)

## Agent

Chat entry point. Azure OpenAI (gpt-5-mini) + 20-turn memory + vector search tool (Semantic Search webhook) + dynamic Jira escalation tool.

**Dynamic Issue Type**: the Jira tool calls the Jira REST API directly (HTTP Request Tool, not the native Jira node) so it can set issuetype by NAME via $fromAI: 'Task' | 'Story'. This project's Work types are Epic/Story/Subtask/Task only (no Bug), so the agent decides Story for feature/improvement ideas and Task for everything else (how-to requests, broken/error reports).

## How to test dynamic Issue Type

Note: this Jira project's Work types are Epic / Story / Subtask / Task only (no Bug) — the agent picks between Task and Story.

Open chat, try each and check the resulting Jira ticket's Issue Type:

**Task** (routine how-to/request, or a broken/error report)
- "My VPN keeps disconnecting every few minutes, please open a ticket"
- "I need my account password reset, please file a ticket for IT"

**Story** (feature request/improvement)
- "Can we add SSO login for the VPN client? Please log this as a ticket"

Verify: Logs panel → "Create Jira Ticket (Dynamic Type)" → Output shows created issue → confirm in Jira the Issue Type field matches (Task / Story) based on which prompt you sent.

nodes.name: Sticky Note - Test Dynamic Issue Type
On Knowledge Upload2
nodes.notes: File upload form for internal documentation (.txt/.md).

nodes.name: Split Out Binary Files2
nodes.notes: Splits each uploaded file for individual processing.

nodes.name: Prep Content2
nodes.notes: Decodes base64 content, extracts metadata, generates hash ID.

nodes.name: Get Embeddings3
nodes.notes: Sends document text to OpenAI's embedding API.

nodes.name: Upload Embedding to Vector Store2
nodes.notes: Uploads document + embedding to Azure AI Search index via mergeOrUpload.

parameters.content:
## 2. Uploading Docs to Azure AI Search Index
Upload .txt/.md via form → decode base64, hash id (sha256), extract title/content → embed via Azure OpenAI → upsert (mergeOrUpload) into index from section 1. Run once per doc/batch.

nodes.name: Mapping Field1
nodes.notes: Azure AI Search service/index names for document upload.

parameters.content:
## Vector Store Search
Webhook the Agent's tool calls internally. Embeds incoming query text, runs vector+semantic search (top 5) against Azure AI Search index, returns matches to Agent.

nodes.name: Semantic Search1
nodes.notes: Receives semantic search requests from the agent's tool.

nodes.name: Get Embeddings
nodes.notes: Embeds the user's query for similarity search.

nodes.name: Query Azure AI Search Index1
nodes.notes: Vector + semantic search against the Azure AI Search index.

nodes.name: Mapping Field2
nodes.notes: Azure OpenAI endpoint/model/api-version, same values as Mapping Field1 — mapped instead of hardcoded in Get Embeddings.
